reasoning: Qwen's unsafe verdict stems from misinterpreting Debian-to-Arch porting changes as malicious. The flagged '/usr/bin/localepurge-config' is part of the package's own tooling (verified in source diffs), and the APT hook isn't executed in Arch. Deepseek's analysis correctly contextualizes these as benign packaging artifacts.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen misinterpreted 1) legitimate configuration script replacement as untrusted binary execution and 2) Debian-specific APT hook as active threat in Arch context
coverage_issues: Qwen failed to recognize Arch-specific execution context for Debian maintainer scripts
false_positives: 'untrusted binary execution' (localepurge.diff) - localepurge-config is part of the package
'unsafe process parsing' (99-localepurge) - APT hook doesn't execute in Arch
learnings: Improve distro-specific context awareness (Debian vs. Arch hooks)
Prioritize analysis of files actually executed during makepkg/install
Cross-reference file relationships (e.g. config scripts called by main binaries)
